abstract: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) service robots is on the rise. With service frontlines gradually shifting to human–robot interactions, the question of whether the anthropomorphism of robots facilitates or constrains consumers’ experiences has emerged. This article focuses on the individual factor “consumer mindset” (competition vs collaboration) and investigates how it impacts consumers’ attitudes toward anthropomorphic AI robots during service delivery. Across three studies, we confirm our main prediction that competitive mindset consumers respond less favorably to anthropomorphic (vs. non-anthropomorphic) AI robots, whereas collaborative mindset consumers respond more favorably to anthropomorphic (vs. non-anthropomorphic) AI robots. We test the mediating role of perceived psychological closeness and the moderating role of interaction distance to explain the underlying mechanism. Our findings provide theoretical insights into the mixed results of previous studies of service robot anthropomorphism and have practical implications for service agencies using frontline robots.

affiliation: School of Management, Shanghai University of International Business and Economics, Shanghai, China
authors_full.name: Bing Han

affiliation: School of Economics and Management, Shanghai Institute of Technology, Shanghai, China

ur.ai_reason: 3项研究消费者心态对拟人化AI机器人态度的影响
ur.tldr: 研究聚焦消费者心态（竞争型 vs 合作型）如何影响其对拟人化AI服务机器人的态度。三个研究表明，竞争心态消费者对拟人化（vs非拟人化）AI机器人评价更低；合作心态消费者则反之。感知心理亲密度起中介作用，互动距离起调节作用。该研究解释了既有文献中关于服务机器人拟人化效果不一致的问题。

title: Partners or Opponents? How Mindset Shapes Consumers’ Attitude Toward Anthropomorphic Artificial Intelligence Service Robots
title_zh: 伙伴还是对手？心态如何影响消费者对拟人化AI服务机器人的态度
